item.title: President of the Assembly of the Republic makes official visit to China and Macau until Saturday
item.description: The President of the Assembly of the Republic is carrying out an official visit to China until Saturday, which includes meetings with Chinese authorities in Beijing and with Portuguese communities in Shanghai, Macau and Hong Kong. According to a note from José Pedro Aguiar-Branco's office, this trip takes place at the invitation of the president of the Standing Committee of the National People's Assembly of the People's Republic of China, Zhao Leji, made during his visit to Portugal in November 2024 and is part of the memorandum of understanding signed in July 2017 by the presidents of the two parliaments at the time. The last official visit of a President of the Assembly of the Republic to China took place in 2018 (when the socialist Ferro Rodrigues held the position) and the Portuguese delegation will include deputies from the Portugal-China Parliamentary Friendship Group from various parties: Hugo Carneiro (PSD), Paulo Núncio (CDS-PP), Edite Estrela (PS), Felicidade Vital (Chega) and Paula Santos (PCP). On Wednesday, April 8, Aguiar-Branco is scheduled to meet with the vice-president of the People's Republic of China, Han Zheng, and with the president of the Standing Committee of the National People's Congress of the People's Republic of China, Zhao Leji, followed by an official dinner. On Thursday, in Shanghai, the president of the Assembly of the Republic participates in a lunch with members of the Portuguese community, visits the Fosun Art Center, an urban planning exhibition center and meets with local authorities and the president of the Standing Committee of the National People's Congress of this municipality, Huang Lixin. The program on Friday and Saturday will be in Macau, where Aguiar-Branco will visit Portugal's stand at the International Tourism Exhibition (MITE) and will have meetings with the chief executive of the Macau Special Administrative Region and the president of the Legislative Assembly of this territory. Also on Friday, Aguiar-Branco will go to the Portuguese School of Macau and, on Saturday, he will visit some emblematic sites of the Portuguese presence in this region, whose administration passed from Portugal to China in 1999: the Santa Casa da Misericórdia, the ruins of São Paulo Cathedral, the Portuguese Bookstore and the General Consulate. Meetings with the Portuguese community, including the legal community, and the inauguration of an exhibition commemorating the 25th of April, entitled “The arts are on the Street” complete the program in Macau. On Saturday afternoon, Aguiar-Branco and the Portuguese delegation will also visit the exhibition “Portuguese Stories”, about the Portuguese presence in this territory, in the Special Administrative Region of Hong Kong, and meet with members of the Portuguese community who live in this former British colony.

item.title: Taiwan | Opposition leader on a “journey for peace”
item.description: The leader of the opposition in Taiwan, Cheng Li-wun, began a visit to China yesterday at the invitation of Chinese President Xi Jinping, in a trip that she described as a “trip for peace”. The visit, the first by a Taiwanese opposition leader to China in around a decade, comes before a meeting scheduled for May, in Beijing, between Xi Jinping and the President of the United States, Donald Trump. Before leaving Taipei, the president of the Kuomintang (Nationalist Party) told journalists that Taiwan must make every effort to avoid war and seize every opportunity to promote peace. “The purpose of this visit to mainland China is precisely to show the world that it is not just Taiwan that unilaterally wants peace,” said Cheng Li-wun. "I believe that, through this peace journey, everyone will be able to further see the sincerity and determination of the Central Committee of the Communist Party of China in resolving any differences between the two sides through dialogue and peaceful exchanges," he added. "No one wants the island to become a battlefield; everyone would take advantage of any opportunity to prevent war from reaching it: preserving peace is preserving Taiwan", said Cheng, in statements collected by the CNA agency at his party's headquarters in Taipei. Dozens of supporters and opponents gathered at Taipei airport, chanting slogans and displaying signs. The 56-year-old opposition leader will visit the eastern province of Jiangsu, Shanghai and Beijing between April 7th and 12th and, according to the Taiwanese press, plans to meet with Xi on the 10th, a meeting that has not yet been confirmed by either party.

item.title: Middle East | Seoul sends representative to guarantee energy supply
item.description: The head of the South Korean presidential office, Kang Hoon-sik, traveled yesterday to the Middle East and Central Asia to guarantee energy supplies, as part of the war against Iran and the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Kang explained at a press conference in Seoul that his delegation is going to Kazakhstan, Oman and Saudi Arabia to guarantee additional imports of oil and naphtha, given the logistical difficulties arising from the conflict in the Middle East. South Korea imports around 70 percent of the crude oil it consumes from the Middle East, and more than 95 percent of this volume transits through Hormuz. The Asian country recently raised its alert to level 3, the second highest, due to the energy security crisis. Kang explained that 54 percent of the naphtha that South Korea consumes is also imported from the region, highlighting the urgency of securing alternative supply routes. The official highlighted the recent agreement in which the United Arab Emirates (UAE) committed to supplying 24 million barrels of crude oil to Seoul. Furthermore, he indicated that the Government is working to guarantee the safety of 26 South Korean ships that remain in the vicinity of the Strait of Hormuz, coordinating measures with shipping companies and international partners to facilitate their safe passage. In addition, Seoul authorities are considering sending five South Korean-flagged ships to the Saudi city of Yanbu, on the Red Sea coast, which would allow them to avoid passing through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Death | Un Chi Iam, painter and wife of Mio Pang Fei, has passed away
item.description: Last Saturday, painter and university professor Un Chi Iam, widow of the now deceased artist and painter Mio Pang Fei, one of the great names in contemporary art in Macau, died. According to a note sent to the newsroom by the couple's daughter, Cristina Mio U Kit, Un Chi Iam was born in Shanghai in 1938, having moved to Macau in 1983. She had a master's degree in Fine Arts from the Nanjing Art Institute, and dedicated herself, like her husband, to fine arts and also to teaching. Un Chi Iam was art editor of the Revista de Cultura of the Macau Cultural Institute and professor of Chinese painting at the Macau Academy of Visual Arts and the Macau Polytechnic Institute. She was also a founding member of the Círculo dos Amigos da Cultura de Macau, as well as an “active leader” in local associations. In terms of exhibitions held, Un Chi Iam held three solo exhibitions, participated in three Macau Biennials and more than 60 group exhibitions internationally. Furthermore, she was a distinguished painter for her work, having received “several outstanding awards in Chinese painting”. According to the same note, “his work combined traditional Chinese ink with modern and Western influences, incorporating seals, engraving and other Chinese techniques”, in order to “expand the language of contemporary Chinese painting”. The late painter is remembered “as an innovator and a dedicated teacher”, with her influence lasting “through her students, publications and the institutions she helped to build”. Choice couple Next to Un Chi Iam was always Mio Pang Fei, also from Shanghai and whose life and work merited, in 2014, a documentary by Pedro Cardeira. As one of the most important names in Chinese contemporary art, Mio Pang Fei was seen as a reference for many, starting with the fact that, at the beginning of his career, he was interested in Western contemporary art, which in the country was seen as anti-revolutionary. During the years of the Cultural Revolution, Mio Pang Fei had to adopt traditional Chinese art and calligraphy, which were more politically and socially accepted, having been persecuted by Maoism, arrested and sentenced to forced labor. In the early 80s, he escaped to Macau and, there, he was able to continue the art he wanted to develop and a new life. Mio Pang Fei created the so-called Neo-Orientalism, with a mix of the arts he has done all his life: traditional Chinese and Western, more contemporary and with a different perspective. The artist passed away in 2020.

item.title: Zarathustra
item.description: We almost always leave our homeland around the age of thirty, that is, we leave our first life towards our own identity, we go to our mountain and then we start again with something inaugural that has to do with the Saturn cycle symbolically referenced as the death of the father. This cycle is twenty-eight years, but thirty years is perhaps its most precise completion. The life that wants to be free must have at this point all the prerogatives that allow it to remain free, autonomous, and perhaps even forgetful of its first nature. In these years, not only is the death of the father an imperative, but the love to come is a vast spell. Let us then begin the struggle to climb the mountain, the solitude that Moses, and even later Jesus, would experience much later, following the paths of the Goat. The father of monotheistic religions printed this theme on the mountain as if it were an eternal prophecy, and thus it has remained intact to this day, as if the path that erases the foot of the first condition disappeared high up among the clouds that the ground cannot see. Persia is full of the best poets the world has seen, and so much so that in the distant reaches of their empire they prayed to them as if they were sacred identities. At the time of Cyrus the Great. What are barbarians doing in this land drawn by fine horses, in complete opposition to the blasphemous oratory of a White House around the boss? It is a heresy and also a gigantic miscalculation, and the fact that we arrived here in this way reveals the abyss in which we are immersed. And Zarathustra, after a decade spent on the mountain, reaches the age of forty, the fearsome number who develops quarantines, lents and crossings, is a man born of himself and his solitude guiding the cycle of the sun before total night was implemented (the old cults of Mithra are still aligned with the distance of his existence) and he is then on the opposite path. He goes down, a future Moses in amazement at what awaits him below, and an angel questions him: - are you going to meet the men? Don't forget to take the whip - At that time he was supported by a queen and his ruler became a follower of Zoroastrianism, his mission took another decade and gave him the fate of seventy-seven years and forty days. Many believe that it had already existed six thousand years ago, long before Xerxes' invasion of Greece, but in any case, nothing is lost for a millennium more or a millennium less in this prodigious phase of civilization. This rhythm of going down and up had already been placated to give Jacob a dream, and then fight with an angel where he would end up in a trough, but from Zoroaster no one mentioned any hallucination or the slightest sign that he had been lame. In Nietzsche's Zarathustra there is an evocative poem that questions us in the deep voice of conscience: / write with your blood that I have never loved those who do so idly... / see how free I feel, see how light I feel / See flying, see flying over... See! There is a god in me who dances. We are threatened with third, fourth and fifth world wars, we are spiritually at war, whoever takes the mountain path again should ask what it means to kill old men with long beards, that the end of everything begins with other guerrillas where we pass by unscathed as if nothing extraordinary were happening. The sorcerer's spell that kills girls with the initial roar leaves the lists of cannibals to the god, in a chilling resurrected manifestation of Baal. Zarathustra still exists to remind us of the boundary between man and beast, he was summoned in the light of our humanity to also put an end to all sacrificial cults, and he was a father of Abraham in order to put an end once and for all to the massacre of the innocent. A Persian poet said that men resist things because they do not know them, Abu Hamid al-Ghasali, which inspired Saint-Exupéry to say another; men love the Earth because it resists them. What can we do in the midst of all this, and who will come to free us from these “salvifics”? You know time, come and save us.

item.title: First Asia-Pacific “Arrive and Drive” event will be in Coloane
item.description: This year, the FIA ​​launched new regional and international championships in the “Arrive and Drive” format, or “turnkey” in the Portuguese term, with the aim of increasing accessibility and participation of young drivers in karting competitions. The Asia-Pacific FIA Karting Championship “Arrive and Drive”, in its first edition, will consist of three rounds, with the first round scheduled for the Coloane Kartdrome. In this “Arrive & Drive” model, drivers do not need to bring their own kart or join a technical team, as the organization provides all the necessary equipment, including fully standardized karts from the OTK Group, identical engines and tires for everyone, as well as technical assistance, preparation and maintenance of the material. This way, participants just have to show up at the circuit and compete, which helps to significantly reduce costs and reduce technical differences between competitors. The championship is made up of the Junior (12–14 years old, drivers born between 2012 and 2014) and Senior (14–18 years old) categories. With this initiative, the FIA ​​aims to reduce financial barriers in international karting for younger drivers, increase the participation of competitors from countries with fewer resources, ensure more balanced competition conditions — at a time when the costs of the sport have been increasing significantly — and identify and develop new talents for motorsport. After the first announcement last December, the FIA ​​Asia-Pacific “Arrive and Drive” Karting Championship calendar was finally ratified at the latest FIA World Council and will be contested across three events. The first race is scheduled for the Coloane Kartdrome, which this year celebrates its thirtieth anniversary, between the 19th and 21st of June. After the race on Macau soil, the competition continues, from the 24th to the 26th of July, at the Zhuzhou International Karting Circuit, in Mainland China. The third and final round, scheduled for September 11th to 13th, will be held at the Madras International Karting Arena, in India. Registration for the championship is open until May 27th, allowing individual drivers to participate without the need for nomination by their respective national sporting authorities. As a reward, the best classified in the Junior and Senior categories qualify directly for the World Cup of their specialty, thus having the opportunity to secure a place in the prestigious “FIA Karting Shootout”. FIA closer The first edition of the FIA ​​Karting World Cup “Arrive & Drive” took place in November last year, in Malaysia, precisely on the weekend of the Macau Grand Prix. In an unusual but noteworthy initiative, Macao SAR was represented at the event by 14-year-old driver Chong Ian Ip and supervised by João Afonso, the driver with the most titles won in Macau karting. In the Junior category, the competition brought together more than fifty participants from all over the world, with Chong finishing in 19th place in the Group B Final. The presence of yet another FIA competition in Macau is not a product of oblivion and follows the line of proximity between the international federation and the Associação Geral Automóvel Macau-China (AAMC). The Extraordinary General Assembly of the FIA ​​will be organized again in Macau on 26 June. The meeting will once again be promoted by the AAMC and is part of the week of FIA conferences that will also take place in the territory.

item.title: Fuels | Price increase limited to half the market value
item.description: China will continue to limit the increase in fuel prices by practically half, prolonging the measures announced in March to try to mitigate the impact of the rise in oil prices. As indicated yesterday in a statement by the National Development and Reform Commission (CNDR, the country's main economic planning body), gasoline prices will rise by 420 yuan per ton, when they should rise by 800 yuan, following “considerable” fluctuations in the market. The price of diesel will rise by 400 yuan per ton, instead of the 770 yuan in the standard calculation. “To mitigate the impact of rising international crude oil prices on the national market, the Government continues to apply control measures over the prices of petroleum derivatives”, indicates the institution on its portal. On March 23, the CNRD announced that it would limit the increases to 1,160 and 1,115 yuan for gasoline and diesel, instead of the 2,205 and 2,120 yuan that it would have to apply given the scale of oil prices. In yesterday's statement, the person responsible for economic planning demands that large state-owned oil companies “organize the production and transportation” of refined products to “guarantee stable supplies” and urges them to “strictly apply” the aforementioned price controls. The statement warns of “severe penalties” against anyone who violates these measures and asks all authorities in the country to “strengthen market supervision and inspection”. Faced with the “de facto” blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, through which 45 percent of the oil it imports passes, China recorded one of the biggest recent increases in fuel prices, which led regulators to intervene to limit its impact on citizens.

item.title: Ukraine | Beijing and Kiev agree requirements for wheat flour export
item.description: China and Ukraine signed on Monday a protocol on sanitary, inspection and quarantine requirements for the export of Ukrainian wheat flour to the Chinese market, in a new step in agri-food cooperation between the two countries. The agreement was signed on Monday by the Chinese ambassador to Ukraine, Ma Shengkun, and the head of the State Service of Ukraine for Food Safety and Consumer Protection, Serhii Tkachuk, the Chinese embassy in Kiev said yesterday, through its official account on the social network WeChat. According to the Chinese embassy, ​​Ma Shengkun positively evaluated agricultural cooperation between Beijing and Kiev, stating that the signing of the protocol “will further expand commercial cooperation in agricultural products between the two countries” and enrich the content of the bilateral strategic partnership. The meeting was also attended by the Ukrainian Deputy Minister of Economy, Environment and Agriculture, Iryna Ovcharenko, who indicated that China is Ukraine's main trading partner and an “important destination” for Ukrainian agricultural exports, expressing confidence that the protocol will “boost new growth” in bilateral trade in this sector.

item.title: Energy | Xi calls for new energy system and strengthened security
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping yesterday defended the construction of a “new energy system” and the strengthening of the country's energy security, in a context of disruptions in the supply of fossil fuels, caused by the conflict in the Middle East. Without directly referring to the war between the United States and Israel against Iran, Xi stressed that China must “accelerate the planning and construction of a new energy system” and reinforce security, at a time when Beijing is investing in the diversification of supply sources, state television CCTV reported. The head of state called for the “active, safe and orderly” development of nuclear energy and the reinforcement of an integrated system of production, supply, storage and commercialization of energy. The statements come after weeks of instability in the Middle East, where the war involving Iran, Israel and the United States has affected maritime traffic and put pressure on the price of oil, due to the de facto blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. According to CCTV, in the first months of the new five-year plan approved in March, which guides the world's second largest economy over the next five years, China has accelerated the construction of new generation energy infrastructure, with the aim of strengthening the supply chain and promoting greener, low-emission development. At the end of February, the installed capacity of wind and solar energy reached 1,880 million kilowatts, an increase of 28.8 percent year-on-year, while electrical production from renewable sources already represents around 40 percent of the national total. Xi Jinping recently stressed that coal remains China's “energy base” and should play a supporting role. Global impact The conflict, escalating since the end of February, has included attacks on energy infrastructure and affected traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, through which, under normal circumstances, around 20 percent of the world's oil and approximately 45 percent of Chinese oil and gas imports transit. The war has already had a direct impact on China, increasing energy and logistical costs, even leading authorities to temporarily intervene in domestic fuel prices. The Chinese Ministry of Foreign Affairs recently indicated that some Chinese ships managed to cross the strait after coordination with the parties involved. Beijing has repeatedly condemned attacks by the United States and Israel against Iran, while stressing the need to respect the sovereignty of the Gulf countries, with which it maintains close political, commercial and energy relations.

item.title: Point of Convergence: Classics, Oblivion and Renaissance at the 15th Macau Literary Festival
item.description: The Macau Literary Festival through the perspective of Harold Bloom By Lam Kongchuen The famous literary critic Harold Bloom, in his reflections on the relationship between literature and the city, never considered the city simply as a backdrop for literature. For him, big cities — Alexandria, London, Paris, New York — remodel each writer who enters their space with an irresistible force. The city imposes an “anxiety of influence”, forcing subsequent creators to seek their own voice in the shadow of the classics. If Bloom were still alive, perhaps he would enter the 15th edition of «The Script Road — Macau Literary Festival» with a different outlook. This great cultural event, which establishes bridges between East and West, Portugal and China, tradition and contemporaneity, illustrates precisely a central theory in the manner of Bloom: when a literary festival aims at «exchange» and «dialogue», is it mobilizing the essence of literature? 1/ The Shadow of Pessanha The tribute paid at this festival to the centenary of the death of the Portuguese poet Camilo Pessanha offers the richest text for a reading in Bloom's style. Pessanha, a symbolist poet who spent the last 30 years of his life in Macau, whose work «Clepsidra» is acclaimed as one of the most exquisite in the Portuguese language, nevertheless occupies a special position in both cultural traditions. Seen through Bloom, Pessanha is precisely that «strong weak» — in the classical Portuguese tradition, he inherited Pessoa's anxiety; in Macau, he faced a Chinese literary tradition that had not yet fully absorbed him. Yao Feng's new work, “Encounter of Extremes”, tells the story of literary exchange between Portugal and China; This narrative of the “encounter” is precisely the myth that Bloom always sought to deconstruct. For Bloom, the true literary encounter is never a measured dialogue, but rather a direct confrontation with misinterpretation, competition and overcoming. Pessanha's poetry deserves to be celebrated not because he managed to «exchange» something, but because he created, on the fringe between two traditions, a unique anxiety — a voice that is neither totally Portuguese nor totally Macanese. The historical tour «Nas Pegadas de Pessanha», organized by the Macau Literature Festival, was led by writer Christopher Chu. This spatial pilgrimage, if analyzed from Bloom's perspective, is not just a reminiscence of the past, but a ritual through which a «late» attempts to alleviate his anxiety of influence by revisiting the physical footprints of a classic. The question is: when we walk through the streets where Pessanha passed, are we talking to him or just looking for consolation for our delay? 2/Books of Women and Secret Language In 1973, Bloom published “The Anxiety of Influence,” which “struck everyone’s nerves,” but his theory of classical training has long been accused by feminist critics of being complicit with patriarchy — the “dominant poets” in his eyes are almost exclusively men, and the “anxiety of influence” he describes is a contest of masculinity. However, this literary festival's focus on “women's writing” offers precisely an alternative to Bloom's analysis. Feng Du's documentary, “The Whisperers”, tells the story of «Nüshu», a script exclusively for women from Jiangyong, in Hunan province — an alphabet created by women to escape male understanding, written on folding fans and embroidered on scarves, which developed as a form of poetry and expression of affection. If Bloom had watched this section, perhaps he would have revised his theory: it is not a female tradition excluded from classical literature that is being “recovered”, but rather a manifestation parallel to classical literature. The very existence of «Nüshu» is a silent protest against dominant classical literature (whether in Chinese or Portuguese) — it does not seek dialogue, it creates its own space. The Literature Festival organized the «Nüshu» workshop and screening on the same day, coinciding with International Women's Day. This curatorial logic attempts to present the “female narrative” as a thematic unit. But a critique of Bloom's style would highlight: the true female literary tradition does not need to be “thematicized”; What it needs is to be recognized as a complete classical system, endowed with internal competition and tradition. When women's literature is brought from the villages of Hunan to the grounds of the Macau Literary Festival, it runs the risk of being transformed into a display of “cultural diversity” rather than a living literary tradition, which continues to generate unrest. 3/ Macau as an urban text: from the point of convergence to the irradiation field This edition of the Literary Festival emphasizes a key word: Macau as a «meeting point for cultures, languages ​​and artistic expressions from East and West». Behind this formulation is what Bloom would call “academic illusion” — as if the encounter between cultures could be harmonious, complementary and mutually enriching, without distinctions. However, Bloom reminds us that true urban literary space is never a meeting place, but rather a battlefield. Paris in the 20th century was not great because it allowed the “encounter” between Hemingway and Joyce, but because it forced each one to search for their own voice in the shadow of the others. Likewise, Macau's literary potential lies not in its ability to “connect” China and the Portuguese-speaking world, but rather in its ability to become a place where new anxieties, new misinterpretations and new classics emerge. The exhibition by architect José Maneiras — a retrospective on this Macanese architect who designed the São Francisco Complex — offers a spatial metaphor. Architecture is the most physical manifestation of the classic: a building stands there, and the architects that follow have to decide whether to imitate it, destroy it or establish a silent dialogue with it. The colloquium organized by the Literature Festival seeks to “pay homage to his contribution to the urban landscape”, a ritual of canonization. But Bloom would ask: who is the “dominant poet” of the world of Macau architecture, capable of reinterpreting José Maneiras and, from there, creating a new urban language? 4/Multidisciplinary Manifestations The lineup for this edition of the Literature Festival truly stars: Pulitzer Prize winner Hernán Díaz, novelist Amitav Ghosh, masters of Chinese literature Bi Feiyu and Xiao Bai. At the same time, the event extends to cinema (A Mulher do Mar), theater (the Portuguese version of À Primeira Vista), music (Rodrigo Leão's concert) and visual arts. Literature manifests itself in multiple domains. On the one hand, there is an effort to broaden the audience for literature; on the other, as the literary festival increasingly becomes a public event, the autonomy of literature is widely mobilized, expanded and disseminated. «À Primeira Vista» is an excellent piece of theater, and actress Margarida Vila-Nova will return to Macau to present it. The play, written by Australian playwright Suzie Miller, has been performed in London, New York and around thirty cities around the world, including Shanghai and Hong Kong. Its version produced in Portugal is in line with the core of the «Macao Literature Festival». The concert by Portuguese musician Rodrigo Leão became a separate chapter in the Sino-Portuguese literary exchange. Bloom spent his life defending aesthetic autonomy — he considered that the value of literature does not lie in its political correctness, cultural representativeness or social utility, but rather in the intensity of its unique and irreducible imagination. From this perspective, the two meetings dedicated to poetry at the festival, the last of which being the highlight of the entire event, constitute the most “Bloomian” moment. Poetry — this medium that has, by nature, the characteristics of «transdisciplinarity» and «exchange», but which, at the same time, is the most obstinately faithful to the language itself — was placed at the end, as if it were a reflection on the outcome of the entire literary celebration. Bloom would say: this is where the literary festival, upon reaching its end, begins again, just as the sea begins at the end of the earth. 5/ Thus, immortal literature «The Immortality of the Word», this name in itself is full of Bloomian irony. What is immortality? For Bloom, immortality is not about being remembered, but about continually generating anxiety — making later writers unable to sleep at night because of your existence, making readers feel, before your words, the limits and transcendence of language. A «ponte cultural» que o Festival de Literatura de Macau procura construir tem a sua necessidade e valor, especialmente numa cidade ainda marcada pela intersecção e coexistência de uma história diversificada, da política linguística e da identidade. Mas Bloom lembrar-nos-ia: o significado da ponte não reside em tornar esta margem e aquela margem idênticas, mas sim em tornar as diferenças atravessáveis, confrontáveis e passíveis de interpretação errada. Pessanha's immortality is not due to the fact that he managed to transmit some information between Portugal and China, but rather to the fact that his poetry refused to be completely absorbed by any tradition — what he left in Macau were not traces of convergence, but rather marks of anxiety. It is continuous anxiety that brings new impulses to literary creation. As we leave Fundação Oriente, ending this eleven-day literary banquet, the real question is not “how much we exchanged”, but rather “whether we generated a new anxiety” — an anxiety capable of propelling a new generation of Macau writers and artists to create, in the shadow of Pissarro, the Book Women, Masua and all these classics, their own, strong and unsettling literary language. Only in this way can Macau, in the literary sense, truly become “immortal”.

item.title: Lisbon | Fourth volume of “Jade Figures” by António Aresta released
item.description: “Jade Figures IV – The Portuguese in the Far East”, a work by António Aresta, former Philosophy professor in Macau, who has written about the culture of the territory, was launched yesterday in Lisbon. The author describes here several personalities who helped tell Macau to the world, confessing that he is already working on the fifth and sixth volumes of the same work Yesterday, in Lisbon, at the Historical Society of the Independence of Portugal (SHIP), the launch of the work “Jade Figures IV – The Portuguese in the Far East”, by António Aresta, who taught Philosophy in Macau between 1987 and 1998 and who, since then, has dedicated himself to writing about the culture of the territory. “Jade Figures IV” is an edition of the International Institute of Macau (IIM), made in 2025, which has now been launched in Lisbon. Speaking to HM, António Aresta said that this volume of the book “shows that the project is still thriving”, having already studied hundreds of Portuguese personalities who helped to showcase Macau in its various aspects. However, the author highlights one: Manuel da Silva Mendes, considering him “the most relevant Portuguese figure”. All thanks to “his intellectual dimension, his civic commitment, his refined aspect as a collector of works of art”, without forgetting the fact that he was “a teacher and rector of the Liceu, councilor of the Leal Senado and a lawyer”. In António Aresta's view, Manuel da Silva Mendes “built, a rare thing among Portuguese expatriates, a showy 'tai pan' mansion, on the Guia hill”, having “unfortunately died prematurely, victim of a disabling illness”. In this session at SHIP, the four already published volumes of “Jade Figures” were presented, which constituted, in the author's view, “an opportunity to return Macau to the center of Portuguese cultural information”. For António Aresta, “the opacity and invisibility of Macau and Chinese culture in Portugal is an oriental mystery”. "Orientalism, Macau studies and sinology are not news or interest for Portuguese journalism, which is a shame. Because our history is so old, very interesting and valuable. Macau is a miracle of understanding between Portuguese and Chinese", he highlighted. More on the way Also according to António Aresta, the name “Jade Figures” refers to the fact that this is “a precious stone so appreciated by the Chinese” and “a great symbol of the art of being Portuguese in Macau and the Far East”. Thus, “the Portuguese are the jade of Macau, without them none of this would exist or make sense”, as “they invented Macau, with the friendship, work and complicity of the Chinese”, he considered. António Aresta says he is working on more volumes of “Jade Figures”, with the fifth “being in print”, and “expected to be presented this year”. The author also says he is working on the sixth volume, considering the work around “Jade Figures” as “infinite” and “dependent on three things: health, the support of Jornal Tribuna de Macau and the invaluable support of IIM”. Long context António Aresta already has many texts about Macau, having already written works such as “O Neoconfuncionismo na Educação Portuguesa”, published in 1996, or even “A Educação Portuguesa no Extremo Oriente”, published in 1999. The author has also edited a book about Manuel da Silva Mendes, which came out in 2017. All these editions and research allowed António Aresta to acquire “a panoramic and multidimensional view of culture in Macau, which is very rich and problematic”. The four volumes already published of “Jade Figures” demonstrate, in the author’s opinion, “that Macau was never indifferent to the Portuguese who studied and were interested in the most varied things”. "Another very curious thing is the charitable and philanthropic aspect. Many of the Portuguese who were in Macau offered what they lovingly collected and purchased, such as libraries, works of art, documentary collections, furniture, photographs, to archives, museums and libraries in Portugal. It is something discreet and forgotten", he concluded.

item.title: From comedy to art: more festivals and new events are coming
item.description: Macau will host, in the coming weeks, a series of cultural activities organized by the Cultural Institute (IC) including, for example, the new edition of a comedy festival. One of the events that brings together the areas of sport and culture takes place this weekend, Saturday and Sunday, the 11th and 12th. The “Cultural and Sports Festival” aims to “deepen the interconnection between the two areas” and takes place at the Nautical Center of Praia Grande, the main venue, and also in several historical places such as the Ruins of S. Paulo, Casa de Lou Kau, Praceta de Venceslau de Morais, Bairro of Ilha Verde and Lok Kuan Building of Seac Pai Van. “Demonstrations and experiences of cultural and sporting projects” will take place here, in addition to stalls under the theme “Culture and Sports Meet in Macau”. The list of experiences includes themes such as Dragon Boats, Cantonese Opera, Portuguese Folk Dance, Wing Chun Martial Arts or climbing. Another of the IC's highlights is the International Comedy Festival, which is now in its third edition and takes place between this Thursday and Sunday, from the 9th to the 12th of April. Organized by Mahua FunAge and Damai, this festival extends to Hengqin and promises to present “a diverse range of performing arts shows”. Of note is the show “Parque da Alegria”, at Anim’Arte NAM VAN, inspired by historical figures from Macau, while in Hengqin there will be a selection of “new talents for Macau’s practical comedy training course”. The lineup of events also includes the holding, from the 23rd of April, of the Tap Seac Spring Crafts Fair, which runs for two consecutive weeks, always from Thursday to Sunday. There are “stands that sell original cultural and creative products from Macau and abroad”. More reading needed Still in the cultural area, the IC will launch the “Macau Lê – National Reading Week 2026” initiative, which will take place between the 20th and 26th of April. The ceremony to begin this activity and the “10 Minutes of Reading” event will take place on the 23rd of this month, while the Macau Cultural Center (CCM) will then host, between the 25th and 26th, “various reading promotion activities, including Book Exchange, Magazine Sales, Joint Family Reading Activities, Games Stands and Artistic Performances”. In July, there will be new exhibitions, such as “Nature Duets: Landscape Paintings from the Ming and Qing Dynasties from the National Museum of China”. The exhibition will be on display at the Macau Museum of Arts (MAM) between the 25th of April and the 26th of July, where “works from the collection of the National Museum of China will be presented, namely landscape paintings from the Ming and Qing dynasties and the feelings of painters”. Also noteworthy is the exhibition “Great Spirit of the Han Dynasty – Exhibition of Treasures of the Han Dynasty of Xuzhou”, taking place between April 18th and June 14th at the Macau Museum. Here “works from the Xuzhou Museum collection are presented, demonstrating a multidimensional vision of the history of the Kingdom of Chu during the Han dynasty”.

item.title: Illegal works | In 72 cases, only two resulted in fines
item.description: The Directorate of Land and Urban Construction Services (DSCCU) says it has inspected, since the entry into force of the new legal regime for urban construction, in 2022, 90 buildings for the existence of illegal constructions. Of this number, 72 cases were initiated, only two of which resulted in fines, both concerning “the closure of balconies”. These numbers, which range from 2022 to March of this year, also show that, in 23 cases, the owners ended up collaborating with the authorities, voluntarily demolishing the illegal works. Of the 90 buildings inspected, the existence of illegal works was found in 21 of these buildings, which resulted in the initiation of 72 proceedings. It should be noted that “around a third of the owners involved have already requested voluntary demolition”. Buses | Career no. 15 starts this week The Directorate of Services for Traffic Affairs (DSAT) announced that the new bus route no. 15 will start operating this week, particularly from Saturday. This route includes, according to a statement, the current routes 15, 15S and 15S1, starting to operate on the route between Jardins do Oceano and Povoação de Ká-Hó, with frequencies reinforced to 22 to 26 minutes. Além disso, a DSAT acrescentou o percurso entre a Estrada do Altinho de Ká-Hó e o Terminal de Combustíveis do Porto de Ká-Hó. Another bus route that underwent changes was number 52, and “passengers coming from the Ocean areas heading to the Concórdia Industrial Park can reach their respective destinations using the adjusted route number 52”, explains DSAT.

item.title: Fire | Focus on Rua do Cunha will have a criminal hand
item.description: The Judiciary Police (PJ) is investigating a fire that broke out at a sales stand on Rua do Cunha, due to suspected arson, on April 4th. The information was released by the PJ and cited by the newspaper Ou Mun. The fire was detected by a passerby who noticed some burning objects on the sales stand. The passerby called the police for help, and the fire was extinguished by firefighters. No injuries were reported, but the case was classified as suspected arson and was handed over to the Judiciary Police for further investigation. Accident | Detained after crashing into metal barriers A local resident was arrested after the vehicle he was driving crashed into metal barriers, on Avenida da Amizade, at around 9:30 pm. According to the outlines presented by the authorities, the accident happened on April 1st, and, at first, the man fled the scene. However, the clash was seen by witnesses, who immediately called the police. Authorities identified the 40-year-old man, who was detained and reported an alcohol content of 1.11 grams per liter of blood. After his arrest, the man confessed to having been drinking at dinner and indicated that when he crashed he was driving to return home. The case was handed over to the Public Ministry.

item.title: Game | Annual revenues could grow up to 8.3 percent
item.description: As the year progresses, analysts begin to correct initial growth estimates for Macau's main industry. After the 14.3 percent increase in revenue in the first quarter, forecasts become even more optimistic Stock valuation company CBRE estimates that the gaming market will grow 8.3 percent throughout this year, which indicates that it is an estimate that exceeds the consensus among analysts by two percentage points. CBRE's most recent report was cited by the GGRAsia portal, after being released on Monday. The estimate by analysts John DeCree and Max Marsh takes into account the 14.3 percent annual increase in revenue in the first quarter of the year, to 65.87 billion patacas. Analyst consensus points to annual growth of 6.0 percent. However, DeCree and Marsh explain that this value implies a slowdown in revenues, given the growth in the first quarter, which is not expected: “after the first quarter, [growth] in revenues would have to slow down to 3.5 percent during the rest of the year… We consider [such] slowdown unlikely”, it is justified. Analysts further state that they “expect Macau’s gross gaming revenues to grow faster” than the underlying Gross Domestic Product of the Interior. In the Interior, GDP growth is expected between 4.5 percent and 5 percent, the lowest value in the last decade, excluding the pandemic period. More moderate April In March, revenues showed an annual growth of 15 percent, to 22.61 billion patacas, very close to January, when revenues grew 24 percent, in annual terms, to 22.63 billion patacas. However, more moderate growth is expected in April, with estimates varying between 10 percent and 12 percent. According to forecasts from investment bank Seaport Research Partners, April is expected to see annual growth of 12 percent. However, from the second half of the year onwards, analyst Vitaly Umansky expects more moderate growth, given that the values ​​achieved in the same period last year are higher. As for JP Morgan, analysts Daniel Politzer, Samuel Nielsen and Michael Hirsh are more moderate, with revenue growth forecasts of 10 percent in April.

item.title: One Oasis | Condominium gives up Transmac services
item.description: The management of the One Oasis condominium has renounced the provision of shuttle services from the company Transmac. The information was released by Canal Macau, which indicates that the new option involved purchasing all the buses that were previously used by the travel agency that provided the service. Since the beginning of the year, with the entry into force of the new law on the activity of travel agencies and the profession of tourist guide, the Government has tightened supervision over the provision of shuttle services by travel agencies. The law was approved by deputies and proposed by the Government. Before the new law, the provision of shuttle services by travel agencies to housing developments was an illegal but common practice.

item.title: Interior travelers choose Macau as their destination of choice
item.description: Alibaba Group's Chinese online travel platform recorded a significant increase in bookings for the Qingming Festival, with Macau continuing to be one of the most popular destinations for Chinese tourists. "Macau is currently one of the fastest growing destinations for Qingming holidays. Hotel and flight bookings are on the rise, while bookings for travel and leisure products, including tour packages, private chauffeur services and attraction tickets, have more than doubled compared to the previous year", the Fliggy platform told Lusa. The Qingming Festival, in which the Chinese clean the tombs of their ancestors, took place between April 4th and 6th. According to information sent by the company, Chinese domestic tourism led travel trends, with hotel bookings growing by 40 percent year-on-year and ticket sales for tourist sites rising by more than 70 percent. Macau and other longer travel regions in the country, such as Hainan, Xinjiang, Yunnan and Tibet, recorded growth of more than 100 percent and established themselves as preferred destinations for travel during this holiday. Large urban centers such as Beijing, Guangzhou and Chengdu led the bookings, but lesser-known destinations also expanded. Purchases of vacation packages on the platform increased by 72 percent in March, while searches for “flower viewing” and “spring tours” soared by almost 380 percent compared to the previous month. Cities such as Shanghai, Hangzhou and Wuhan have organized flower-themed festivals to attract visitors. Out Travel abroad was also on the rise, with spending on leisure activities – from boat trips to helicopter excursions – increasing 120 percent year-on-year. Short-haul destinations, less than a four-hour flight, such as South Korea, Thailand and Singapore, remain popular. The platform also recorded very rapid growth, with strong demand from countries such as Sri Lanka, New Zealand, Iceland and Greece. In the first two months, Macau received 7.82 million visitors, an increase of 15.1 percent compared to the same period in 2025. The territory reached the 10 million visitor mark on March 21, reaching this number 12 days earlier than in 2025. Local authorities predict that the year will end with 41 million visitors.

item.title: Banking | Profits rise 14.3 percent in two months
item.description: Despite the AMCM having approved three cuts in the reference interest rate in 2025, banks are managing to generate more money, with profits in the first two months of the year reaching 2.71 billion patacas Macau banks made a profit of 2.71 billion patacas in the first two months of the year, 14.3 percent more than in the same period last year. According to official data from the Macau Monetary Authority (AMCM), released on Thursday, the main reason for the rise in profits was an increase of 24.4 percent, to 3.09 billion patacas, in the interest margin, the difference between loan income and deposit expenses. This is despite the AMCM having approved three cuts in the main reference interest rate in 2025, the last of which was a cut of 0.25 percentage points, introduced on December 11, following the North American Federal Reserve. Loans, the main source of banking revenue worldwide, rose 0.3 percent compared to February 2025, standing at 1.05 billion patacas. But deposits with Macau banks increased even more, 7.4 percent, to 1.42 billion patacas at the end of last February, said AMCM. Macau banks made a profit of 7.34 billion patacas in 2025, almost double that recorded in the previous year (92.7 percent more). The most profitable year ever for banks in the Chinese special administrative region was 2020, when profits were close to 17 billion patacas. Macau has two currency-issuing banks: the local branch of the Chinese state bank Banco da China and Banco Nacional Ultramarino (BNU), which belongs to the Caixa Geral de Depósitos Group. BNU announced at the beginning of February net profits of 431.2 million patacas, 153.9 million patacas less than in 2024, something the bank attributed to the fall in interest rates. Poorly broken Non-performing loans fell for the third consecutive month, to 48.8 billion patacas, after having shrunk 11.6 percent in 2025, the first annual drop since 2013. Overdue loans represented 4.7 percent of Macau banks' loans, 0.8 percentage points less than in February 2025. A percentage that rises to 5.1 percent in the case of credit to institutions or individuals outside the Chinese region. The European Banking Authority, the EU regulatory agency, for example, considers that banks with at least 5 percent of non-performing loans have “high exposure” to risk and must establish a strategy to resolve the problem.

item.title: UCCLA Assembly marked by solidarity in the face of natural disasters
item.description: Solidarity with the populations affected by the rains in Mozambique, Cape Verde and Portugal will mark the XLIII general assembly of the Union of Portuguese Speaking Capital Cities (UCCLA), which brings together the cities and companies that are members of the organization on the 13th in Macau. In addition to the discussion and approval of three motions relating to the intense rains in Mozambique at the end of December 2025 and during January of this year, in Cape Verde in August 2025, and at the beginning of 2026 in Portugal, events all marked by the occurrence of fatalities and extensive losses, the assembly's agenda also includes a vote of condolence and recognition for the writer António Lobo Antunes, who died on the 5th of March. The UCCLA main meeting, which this year takes place in Macau, at the Services Platform Complex for Commercial Cooperation between China and Portuguese-Speaking Countries (Forum Macau), will also consider applications for the organization of the city of Viseu, as well as the Angolan province of Ícolo and Bengo, south of Luanda, which covers seven municipalities, and also a Portuguese-Brazilian association. On the sidelines of the general assembly, the following day, a business forum is planned to be held under the theme “Infrastructures and Smart Cities”, which aims to reinforce “the economic-commercial dynamics of the institution”, according to a statement from UCCLA. Promote investment The event will allow the host city to develop as a sub-theme the “investment environment in Macau, the advantages of the Sino-Lusophone platform, and the joint development of Macau and the In-depth Cooperation Zone between Guangdong and Macau in Hengqin and the business opportunities brought by the Greater Bay Area to Portuguese-speaking countries, from the point of view of external investors and possible Portuguese-speaking partner companies”, according to the statement. Once the forum has concluded, the Government of Macau offers participants a guided tour of Hengqin, a special economic zone under a special cooperation regime between the city of Zhuhai and Macau, where it aims to publicize the possibilities of exchange with services and companies in the interior of China. UCCLA is an intermunicipal, non-profit organization dedicated to promoting exchange and cooperation between its members in various areas. Established on June 28, 1985, UCCLA has among its founding cities Bissau, Lisbon, Luanda, Macau, Maputo, Praia, Rio de Janeiro and São Tomé/Água Grande. It currently has 106 members, including 24 members, 44 associates, 28 supporters and 10 observers.

item.title: Optoelectronics | UN will open Sino-Portuguese laboratory in Macau
item.description: The inauguration of the new space, created jointly by Universidade Nova de Lisboa and the University of Science and Technology of Macau, with a focus on the study and application of electronic devices that supply, detect and control light, takes place tomorrow The Universidade Nova de Lisboa (UNL) will inaugurate this Thursday, in Macau, a Sino-Portuguese laboratory to study “sustainable energy, cutting-edge screens and smart devices”, scientist Elvira Fortunato told Lusa. The new institution will bring together the Institute of Nano-structures, Nano-modeling and Nano-fabrication (i3N) at UNL and the Institute of Materials Science and Engineering at the Macau University of Science and Technology (MUST). The Joint Sino-Portuguese Optoelectronics Laboratory will bring together “specialized knowledge in optoelectronics, nanotechnology and advanced materials from both sides”, explained Fortunato. Optoelectronics is the study and application of electronic devices that supply, detect and control light, including the computers of the future, which will be able to work with light and not just with electronic transitions. The objective is to be “a space where researchers from Portugal, China and other countries can work (…) on common challenges, such as sustainable energy, cutting-edge screens and smart devices”, added Fortunato. “Macau, with its unique history as a meeting point between East and West, is a natural place for this type of collaboration”, highlighted the former Portuguese Minister of Science, Technology and Higher Education (2022-2024). Fortunato highlighted that the joint laboratory “represents the culmination of a long and fruitful partnership” between the two universities, which at the end of 2025 signed a new cooperation agreement. Support from Macao SAR The agreement had the support of the Government of Macau and the new laboratory already has funding from the Fund for the Development of Sciences and Technology. This “sends a clear signal that the Macau Government is committed to developing a world-class research capacity and transforming scientific discoveries into real-world impact”, he added. Elvira Fortunato and her husband, Rodrigo Martins, who has led the European Academy of Sciences since 2018, are known for having invented, in 2008, with colleagues, the so-called “electronic paper”, the first transistor made of paper. Martins, coordinator of i3N-NOVA, highlighted that the institute is also developing another research project in partnership with MUST on “so-called advanced functional materials” for energy. In parallel with the inauguration of the laboratory, MUST will host a three-day forum on optoelectronic materials, in collaboration with Suzhou University in eastern China. In a note sent to Lusa, MUST highlighted that “this laboratory is the first of its kind in Macau and the first China-Portugal joint research laboratory (…) dedicated to optoelectronics”.

item.title: Gender Identity | Changes only with “prudent study”
item.description: The Macau authorities declared to Lusa the need to carry out a “prudent study on the legal issues involved” in the recognition of gender identity, ruling out any legislative review. “In a situation where there is no general consensus in society, the Macao SAR Government still needs to carry out a prudent study on the legal issues involved”, responded by email to the Directorate of Justice Affairs Services (DSAJ). Lusa had questioned this department about whether it planned to create legislation for the recognition of gender identity in Macau, the only place in China where there are no laws that allow residents to change the gender marker on their identity document or undergo sexual reassignment surgery. “The recognition of gender identity involves issues, namely related to the core value of Macau society, the provisions of civil law and marriage and family relationships”, the DSAJ also indicated. This department stated that, “to date, there is no plan to review the respective legal regime”. Four trans people in Macau told Lusa, on the occasion of International Transgender Day of Visibility, marked on March 31, a journey of exclusion, with barriers in access to medical care, employment and education.

item.title: Energy | Request greater control over suppliers
item.description:
Representative linked to the Residents, Leong Hong Sai, is concerned about the “persistent instability in the Middle East region” and the consequences of inflation for the population and small and medium-sized companies Deputy Leong Hong Sai argues that the Government should increase control over energy suppliers. The position was taken through a written question from the legislator linked to the Residents' Association, at a time when the world economy continues to deal with the consequences of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. “Persistent instability in the Middle East region has caused severe disruptions in global energy markets, with a sharp rise in international energy prices, profoundly affecting energy supplies, the functioning of industries and the well-being of the population of all economies in the world”, writes the deputy. “Energy supply is directly linked to social stability and the quality of life of the population in general, and the increase in international energy prices also exerts latent pressure and challenges on Macau”, warns the Executive. “Price stability is in the interests of all residents, especially with regard to protecting the livelihoods of the most vulnerable groups, while the ‘gasoline to electricity’ process constitutes a fundamental step towards reducing dependence on fossil fuels and achieving sustainable development”, he adds. In this sense, Leong Hong Sai wants to know how “the Government will regulate energy suppliers” in areas such as natural gas, butane gas, gasoline and electricity. The legislator also wants to know whether the Executive will publish “more data on prices” and “closely monitor price variations”.
Support in sight? Given the possibility of fuels increasing the prices charged by small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), further reducing competitiveness, especially in the interior, Leong Hong Sai wants the Government to explain whether there is an opening to support the local economy. “The increase in the cost of energy will certainly affect the commercial environment of small and medium-sized companies and the standard of living of the population, therefore, how will the Government define, according to the real situation, special measures to support small and medium-sized companies and families in vulnerable situations, with a view to ensuring the stability of the population's life?”, he asked. Finally, the deputy sees the energy shock as an opportunity to diversify energy sources and invest in alternatives. Specifically, the deputy argues that combustion vehicles should be abandoned, and focus on electric vehicles, a market in which the Interior is the main producer, and also “explore photovoltaic energy”.

item.title: Money Laundering | Macau remains a “key node for money laundering”
item.description: The scenario was outlined by money laundering experts at Agência Lusa, who highlight that the business takes on new forms, after the campaigns against junket companies. The new models include exchange offices, pawnshops and the use of credit cards Organized crime experts told Lusa that Macau continues to be a “fundamental node for money laundering” by criminal organizations, despite the dismantling of the previous VIP gaming system in the territory. According to Martin Pubrick, a former member of the Hong Kong Royal Police and an expert on corruption and organized crime, although Beijing has dismantled the system of VIP gambling solicitors, known as 'junkets', that once dominated the city's casino sector, Macau continues to be a crucial gateway for money laundering in the region. “Although large Chinese criminal syndicates have moved operations across Southeast Asia in response to repressive measures, Macau continues to be an operational and meeting point for these deeply rooted networks,” he told Lusa. The junket system allowed “huge capital flows from mainland China” and enriched groups linked to the triads, a “process that ended in the years before the Covid-19 pandemic, when the central government expanded the anti-corruption campaign, strengthened capital controls and prosecuted several junkets in Macau to send a clear message”, explained the analyst. Alvin Chau Cheok Wa, former head of the city's largest junket group, Suncity Group, was arrested in 2021 and later sentenced to 18 years in prison for criminal association and illicit gambling. In a parallel process, Levo Chan, leader of the Tak Chun Group, the second largest junket in Macau, was sentenced to 13 years in prison on similar charges. Away models John Wojcik, senior researcher at Infoblox Threat Intelligence and former analyst at the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime, makes the same analysis. Although the message from local authorities is clear that “the old ways of business are over”, Macau maintains strategic importance in this parallel industry, he told Lusa. The business model of Macau's junkets and associated criminal groups was, until 2019, centered on casinos. Today, however, activity has shifted to illegal online betting and gaming, with cryptocurrency payments and a significant expansion into large-scale digital fraud schemes, Pubrick noted. “Currency exchange offices, pawn shops and transactions via credit cards have absorbed this demand, which could mean that money laundering in Macau is less centralized and less visible today,” noted the analyst. But “Macau continues to be a key node for money laundering”, he reiterated. These criminal groups, Pubrick described, adapted, moving operations to jurisdictions with weaker regulations, such as Cambodia, the Philippines, which hosted several offshore online gambling companies, as well as border areas of Myanmar (formerly Burma). Many migrated to “illegal online betting, digital fraud and cryptocurrency transactions”, creating a “geographically distributed” business, with victims today expanding to Europe and the Americas. “Macau is no longer the center of money laundering, but it is a regulated gateway that continues to be exploited by criminal groups from Mainland China, Hong Kong and Taiwan”, reinforced Pubrick. Most recent cases Two recent cases illustrate this reality. In March, Taiwanese authorities arrested 20 people involved in a money laundering scheme estimated at 33 billion Taiwan dollars. Among the two dozen detainees, 10 were, however, accused by the Taiwan Public Prosecutor's Office for, in particular, using Macau casinos to launder money from illegal gambling on the Internet. “Money mules” inflated credit limits, purchased large quantities of tokens and exchanged them for local currency, disguising the funds. Two alleged leaders of the scheme remain on the run. In a second case, Lin Ping-wen, a Taiwanese fugitive linked to one of the biggest illegal gambling scandals on Formosa Island and wanted for illegal gambling and money laundering, was shot dead in Cambodia, in Sihanoukville, a coastal city in the southeast of the country known for its casinos. With a background in organized crime in Taipei, Lin was also linked to a baseball match fixing scandal in 2007 and later joined the VIP gambling promotion sector in Macau, with links to the Suncity Group. Official data shows annual reduction in the number of suspicious transactions Last year, the number of suspicious transactions recorded by the Financial Information Office (GIF) showed an annual reduction of 6.1 percent, to 4,925 reports. However, this was the second highest figure in the last 10 years and represents an average of 13 suspicious transactions daily. Most reports are not considered suspicious transactions, and last year only 118 operations were forwarded to the Public Prosecutor's Office. The record number of transactions reported by different sectors of the economy to GIF reached its peak in 2024, when 5,245 financial transactions that generated suspicion were recorded, an average of 14 suspicious transactions per day, with 142 being presented to the Public Prosecutor's Office. In the previous year, in 2023, the number had been 4,614 transactions. Between 2020 and 2022, during the years of the pandemic and strong restrictions on movement in the territory, due to the adoption of the policy of zero cases of covid, the numbers reached lower values, which varied between 2,224 and 2,199, always less than 2,500 transactions. Before the pandemic and until 2016, the highest value had been recorded in 2018, when 3,716 transactions were reported, a value that was only surpassed in 2023, the first time that the record exceeded the barrier of 4,000 transactions, which represents an average of 11 cases per day. More alert game The main complaints received by GIF come from the gaming sector, which last year was responsible for 73.1 percent of the 4,925 reports of suspicious transactions, which meant 3,603 reports. However, the gaming sector submitted fewer reports to GIF in annual terms, given that in 2024 3,837 suspicious transactions were reported. Still with regard to 2025, the second sector to present the most complaints was financial institutions and insurance companies with 1,008 suspicious movements. Finally, all other institutions in the economy submitted 314 reports, which represented 6.4 percent of the total.
